title: Spicy Canadian Pork Roast
description: A flavorful and spicy pork roast recipe, inspired by Canadian cuisine.
instructions: Season the pork shoulder with spicy Montreal seasoning.
Heat olive oil in a large Dutch oven over medium-high heat.
Sear the pork shoulder until browned on all sides, about 5 minutes.
Remove the pork from the pot and set aside.
Add the sliced onion, carrot, and celery to the pot and cook until the vegetables are tender, about 10 minutes.
Add the beef broth, apple cider vinegar, and brown sugar to the pot.
Return the pork shoulder to the pot and cover with a lid.
Transfer the pot to the preheated oven and roast for 2 1/2 to 3 hours, or until the pork is tender and falls apart easily.
Add the potatoes to the pot and continue to roast for an additional 30 minutes.
Remove the pot from the oven and let rest for 10 minutes before slicing the pork and serving.
Serve the pork with the roasted potatoes and vegetables.
